title: Stable
excerpt: A stable creature at 0 hit points is no longer at immediate risk of dying from that collapse and stops making death saving throws, though it remains unconscious until it recovers.
markdown:
# Stable

A stable creature at 0 hit points is no longer in the immediate death-save crisis caused by that collapse. The creature stops making death saving throws, but it remains unconscious.

## Rules Use

Being stable halts the ongoing slide toward death without restoring ordinary action or awareness. Stability means the creature's condition has stopped worsening on its own for the moment.

## Relationship To Recovery

A stable creature remains at 0 hit points until healing or some other effect changes that state. If left alone, the creature can eventually wake naturally after enough time has passed, but it is not combat-ready while it remains unconscious at 0 hit points.

## Losing Stability

Damage or another worsening effect can end the stable state and return the creature to immediate danger, including renewed death saving throw progression if the creature is still at 0 hit points.
